content.full_text:
872D00

July 12, 1976

A-960-433

Honorable Lewis E. Lercara (Retired)
758 Trestle Glen Road
Oakland, California 94610

Dear Judge Lercara:

Thank you for your letter of July 2, requesting information
concerning your possible reporting requirements under the
Political Reform Act of 1974.

The article in the California Courts Commentary, July, 1976
issue, I believe refers to recent legislation, Assembly Bill
809, which requires all judges and district attorneys to file
Statements of Economic Interests with the Fair Political
Practices Commission. Assembly Bill 809 imposes certain
filing obligations on judges and district attorneys who are
currently in office, elected, appointed or leave office, after
the effective date of the bill, May 5, 1976.

Since you retired from office on December 31, 1974, you have
no filing obligations pursuant to this provision of the law.

Very truly yours,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

Superior Court
State of California
COUNTY OF ALAMEDA
COURTHOUSE, OAKLAND 94607

Judge Lewis E. Lercara
758 Fresno Glen Rd.
Oakland, Calif. 94610

July 2, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance/Administration Section
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

In reading an article in the California Courts Commentary
under issue July 1976, for the first time I became familiar
with the fact that under the Political Reform Act of 1974,
certain public officials have been subjected to certain
reporting requirements. Upon reading the article, I could
not arrive at a conclusion as to whether it applied to me
personally or, if so, what steps I should take to obtain
necessary forms, etcetera.

I retired as a Judge of the Superior Court of the State of
California in and for the County of Alameda on December 31,
1974. Subsequent to that time, I again became a member of
the Bar Association and my present status is that of an
attorney at law.

The article I read states "retiring judges file within 30
days of leaving office". Of course, this would be impossible
in my case.

I should appreciate having you advise me at your earliest
convenience as to what my situation is relative to this Act
and what steps, if any, I should take to avoid violation of
the law.

I should appreciate a prompt reply.

Yours truly,

JUDGE LEWIS E. LERCARA (Retired)

The few legible fragments visible include partial words and phrases, but attempting to transcribe them would result in significant gaps and uncertain readings. A clearer or higher contrast image would be necessary for accurate transcription of this document.

Assembly Bill 808 Imbalance Control Districts Commission is the only district structure whose specific objective is to protect any district structure who are financially in office, seeking, lobbying or lance office after filling objective one of these any district structure who are Districts Commission.
